module_description: Students learn the foundations of algebra through story-based conceptual teaching, progressive quizzes, and a final mastery challenge.

final_challenge.title: Algebra Detective Challenge
final_challenge.description: Students solve a final mystery using all Module 1 concepts.

screens.title: The Mystery Box
screens.objective: Students discover unknown values and learn to solve hidden quantity problems.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: I added 2 laddoos to this jar. Now there are 5.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: Can you figure out how many laddoos were there before?

story_segments.speaker: Arjun
story_segments.text: If adding 2 made 5, then there must have been 3 before!

story_segments.speaker: Narrator
story_segments.text: This hidden starting number is called an unknown value.

concept_explanation.title: Understanding Unknown Values
concept_explanation.main_rule: Unknown values are hidden quantities that can be solved by reversing operations.

quiz_questions.question_text: A basket had some mangoes. 4 more were added. Now there are 9. How many were there at the start?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Work backwards from the final total.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which situation contains an unknown value?
options: A box has some coins and 3 more are added to make 10
quiz_questions.correct_answer: A box has some coins and 3 more are added to make 10
quiz_questions.feedback_correct: Correct! The starting value is hidden.

quiz_questions.question_text: Meena Aunty's jar had some biscuits. She added 6. Now it has 10. What was the starting number?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Reverse the addition to find the starting amount.

common_misconceptions: Using final value as initial value

screens.title: Why Variables Were Invented
screens.objective: Students understand variables as symbols representing changing quantities.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: Every laddoo costs 10 rupees, but customers buy different amounts.

story_segments.speaker: Arjun
story_segments.text: Instead of calculating every case separately, let's use n for number of laddoos!

story_segments.speaker: Narrator
story_segments.text: 10n means total cost for any number of laddoos.

story_segments.speaker: Narrator
story_segments.text: Variables help us write one rule that works for many situations.

concept_explanation.title: What Are Variables?
concept_explanation.main_rule: Variables are symbols that represent values that can change.

quiz_questions.question_text: Each pencil costs 15 rupees. Which expression shows total cost?
What does x stand for in 20x?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: x tracks the number of items.

options: The cost of buying any number of samosas
quiz_questions.correct_answer: The cost of buying any number of samosas
quiz_questions.feedback_correct: Correct! Variables help when quantities change.

quiz_questions.question_text: If n = 3 in 10n, what is the total?

screens.title: Meet Constants

story_segments.speaker: Arjun
story_segments.text: In 10n, n changes depending on the customer. But the 10 always stays the same.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: Exactly! The fixed number that never changes is called a constant.

concept_explanation.title: What Are Constants?
concept_explanation.main_rule: Constants are fixed numerical values that do not change.

quiz_questions.question_text: In the expression 8x, which part is the constant?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: The constant is the fixed numerical value.

quiz_questions.question_text: In 6y + 3, how many constants are there?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Look for all fixed numerical values.

common_misconceptions: Thinking only standalone numbers are constants

screens.title: Meet Terms
screens.objective: Students identify terms in algebraic expressions.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: A customer bought 3 boxes of laddoos and paid 5 rupees packaging fee.

story_segments.speaker: Arjun
story_segments.text: That means the bill can be written as 3x + 5.

story_segments.speaker: Narrator
story_segments.text: Each separate part separated by plus or minus signs is called a term.

concept_explanation.title: What Are Terms?
concept_explanation.main_rule: Terms are the individual parts of an expression separated by addition or subtraction.

quiz_questions.question_text: How many terms are in the expression 3x + 5?
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Count the parts separated by plus or minus signs.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of these is NOT a term?
quiz_questions.feedback_correct: Correct! Plus signs separate terms but are not terms themselves.
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Terms are meaningful algebraic parts, not separators.
Separate by plus signs to count terms.

quiz_questions.question_text: In x + y + 8, which is the full list of terms?
quiz_questions.feedback_correct: Correct! All meaningful parts are terms.
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Ignore plus signs when listing terms.

screens.title: Like Terms and Unlike Terms
screens.objective: Students classify and group like and unlike terms.

story_segments.speaker: Meena Aunty
story_segments.text: I have 3 laddoo jars, 5 more laddoo jars, and 2 barfi jars.

story_segments.speaker: Arjun
story_segments.text: We can combine laddoo jars together, but barfi jars stay separate!

story_segments.speaker: Narrator
story_segments.text: Terms with the same variable are called like terms. Different variables are unlike terms.

concept_explanation.title: Like Terms
concept_explanation.main_rule: Like terms have identical variables and powers. Unlike terms have different variables.

quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Like terms must share the same variable.
The variable matters, not the coefficient.

quiz_questions.feedback_correct: Correct! Different variables mean unlike terms.
quiz_questions.feedback_incorrect: Same coefficients do not make terms alike.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is a like term for 3x?
